original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Allows Admin to Steal 100% of LP Fees
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The setReferralFee function in GaugeFactoryCL lacks an upper bound check for dibsPercentage. The REFERRAL_FEE_DENOMINATOR is 1000. If a compromised or malicious admin sets the fee to 1000, 100% of the LP trading fees claimed by the gauge will be routed to the dibs address, leaving nothing for the internal bribe (voters). If set > 1000, the fee calculation will underflow, reverting all fee claims and permanently locking LP fees in the Algebra pool.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Lack of bounds checking on fee parameters is a severe centralization risk. It allows an admin to instantly rug pull all generated trading fees or DoS the fee distribution system.
preconditions: Admin key is compromised or admin acts maliciously
Admin calls setReferralFee with a value >= 1000

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin sets referralFee = 1000 to route all fees to dibs address
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker (admin) extracts 100% of trading fees generated by CL pools

economic_questions: Is the admin key secured by a timelock or multisig?

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: 100% of all CL pool trading fees

market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: Right before a large fee distribution
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Complete loss of LP fee yield for voters

potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All trading fees generated by Algebra CL pools

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
